name: open
why: Start session, load vault context, surface open threads and priorities

name: close
why: End session, commit memory, write daily note + SESSION_LOG entry

name: brief
why: Generate a structured delegation brief for a sub-agent

name: decide
why: Run a decision through the 12-lens evaluation framework

name: setup
why: First-run interview that configures aigent-OS for a new user

name: deep-recon
why: Multi-agent reconnaissance (Explorer/Associator/Critic/Synthesizer)

name: semantic-search
why: Vault search by meaning via local embeddings

name: caddy-enroll
why: Add a new skill to Caddy's index so it can be surfaced by prompt match

name: system-check
why: Audit aigent-OS install — verifies kernel, skills, hooks, daemons, vault, semantic search. Reports green/yellow/red.

name: skill-audit
why: List skills by usage, flag dead ones for retirement, detect Caddy trigger mismatches, surface missing skills.

name: diagnose
why: Walk the seven-layer aigent-OS stack to identify which layer is causing an issue. Caddy pattern applied to debugging.

name: envelope
why: Classify any directive as Ship / Ask one focused question / Propose first. Replaces multi-option surveys with sharp decisions.

name: self-review
why: Spawn reviewer subagent with 4-6 specific verification questions before declaring non-trivial work done.

name: honesty-check
why: Run the volunteering ledger before declaring done — what changed/didn't, noticed-but-not-fixed, tradeoffs, guesses, things stopped short of.

name: first-10-moves
why: Operational recon sequence for unfamiliar codebases. Read first, ask never. 10-step sequence replaces 'survey-mode' with 'read-mode'.

name: stuck
why: 6-rung escalation ladder before declaring stuck. Try alternatives â†’ helper â†’ re-read request â†’ ask. Stopping is last resort.

name: timeline-calibration
why: SELF-CORRECT timeline estimates. About to quote hours/days/weeks? STOP. Divide by 30-60x. Estimate in tool calls and session cycles, not human-dev hours.

name: sandbox-routing
why: SELF-CORRECT tool choice. Use sandbox tools (ctx_execute / ctx_execute_file) for analyzing large outputs. Read/Bash send raw output into context; sandbox sends only the summary.

name: learn
why: Capture a learning sweep as a structured note in vault/concepts/learning/. Categorize, locate on the modern AI stack, assess relevance, capture reconsideration triggers.

name: caddy-explain
why: Walk the deterministic Caddy scoring for a prompt — which trigger matched, what points, what threshold, what near-misses. Pairs with the legibility thesis.

name: caddy-mute
why: Suppress Caddy hints for a bounded duration. Auto-restores. Respects flow without breaking the framework's advisory discipline.

name: caddy-audit
why: Quarterly hygiene check. Walks skill-index vs SKILL.md files on disk. Reports orphans (in index/not on disk and vice versa) and trigger drift.

name: promote
triggers: this should be a vault note
why: Take a slice of the conversation and elevate it to a durable vault artifact (concept / project / person / decision). Selective elevation, not transcript saving.

name: trust-decay
why: Two-phase ledger of confident agent claims and their eventual outcomes. Phase 1 captures the claim; Phase 2 resolves right or wrong. Builds calibration data over time.

name: scout-vault
why: Echo's read-only graph walk. Find notes related to a topic, trace wikilink chains, inventory a directory.

name: comms-summarize
why: Echo's comms polling. Read inbox/channel/thread, return structured unread summary — sender, topic, action, urgency.

name: port-spec
why: Lyra's spec-port skill. Takes a complete written specification and ports it into working code or vault artifact with honesty ledger.

name: ship-adapter
why: Lyra's adapter-shipping skill. Ports a schema, interface, or API contract — DB adapters, API client wrappers, data transformers.

name: research-deep
why: Newton's multi-source research. Vault prior art + web + GitHub synthesized into citation-dense briefing with hypothesis, evidence, recommendation.

name: tool-evaluation
why: Newton's tool evaluation. Assesses tool/library/framework against your needs — alternatives, adoption risks, fit score, recommendation.

name: sprite-spec
why: Iris's sprite spec. Palette, proportions, silhouette, accessories, animation states, frame counts, AI image-gen prompts.

name: visual-polish
why: Iris's UI polish review. Audits an existing UI surface against palette, proportion, hierarchy, motion. Spec output, no code.

name: ui-architecture
why: Iris's new UI surface design. Component tree, layout grid, interaction states, breakpoints, tokens. Spec output, no code.

name: supersede
why: Mnemosyne's OpenChronicle supersede. Strikes outdated entries, adds new with date, preserves history, flags what was kept and why.

name: compact-note
why: Mnemosyne's note compaction. Distills high-churn bloated note into standing-rule summary + truncated log. Preserves signal, removes noise.

name: critique-plan
why: Hypatia's adversarial pre-commitment review. Strongest counterargument first, hidden assumptions surfaced, alternatives named.

name: find-weakness
why: Hypatia's targeted weakness-finding. For a specific claim/architecture/decision: single strongest objection + hidden assumption most likely wrong.

name: sweep-tracker
why: Hestia's delegation tracker sweep. Closes finished items, archives stale items, flags orphaned items. Edit-only on status fields.

name: sweep-heat
why: Hestia's HEAT_INDEX dormant flip. Finds cold bottom-20 notes and sets status:dormant in their frontmatter. Edit-only.

name: sweep-links
why: Hestia's broken wikilink scan. Finds [[wikilinks]] pointing to non-existent files. Reports source file and line. Read-only.

name: orient
why: Load the canonical MAP when confused about geography, ownership, or rules.

name: body-check
why: Compose the AIgent's body state by reading existing signals. Lazy compute. The 'how am I doing?' read.

name: digest
why: Background curation tier. Reviews staged memory candidates from MEMORY_CANDIDATES.md and surfaces promote/skip/supersede choices.

name: context-capsule
why: Structured state preservation. Fires on context pressure OR task completion. Output is a resume-ready capsule, not a vibes summary.

name: start
why: Day-one first-run flow + everyday home screen: on first launch greet, run the operator-setup interview, carry the operator to their first win; on return give a short briefing + plain-English action menu.

name: operator-setup
why: AIgent first-run operator-business interview — learns the operator's business in 3 plain questions, writes the first memory, plays back a personal briefing (the 'it remembers me' moment). Layers on top of the base /setup; invoked by /start, not a replacement.

name: first-win
triggers: show me what you can do
why: Day-one first-win menu — 3 dead-simple high-value tasks (2 zero-dependency) that each put a real, usable artifact in the operator's hands in minutes, satisfying the 'ship something real, fast' promise on day one.

name: impeccable
why: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Avoids generic AI aesthetics. Use for web components, pages, posters, or any design-forward output.

name: frontend-slides
why: Build cinematic HTML/CSS slide decks with bold templates and animation presets. MIT license — Zara Zhang.

name: taste-skill
why: Senior UI/UX engineer persona with metric-based design rules, CSS hardware acceleration, and balanced design engineering. MIT license — LeonxInx.
